texts.label: text
texts.orig: How to take advantage of the increased CO 2 pressure?
texts.text: How to take advantage of the increased CO 2 pressure?

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Reduce reboiler temperature: Little or no saving in energy but in exergy
texts.text: Reduce reboiler temperature: Little or no saving in energy but in exergy

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b)  Increase pressure in stripper and maintain reboiler temperature: Improves CO 2 /H 2 O ratio in vapour leaving stripper, thereby reducing stripping steam requirement
texts.text: Increase pressure in stripper and maintain reboiler temperature: Improves CO 2 /H 2 O ratio in vapour leaving stripper, thereby reducing stripping steam requirement

table_cells.text: • Chemicals expected to have NO environmental effects • PLONOR list
• Include compounds which based on their characteristics are not defined as RED or BLACK, and • NOT included in the PLONOR list

• Inorganic chemicals with high toxicity (EC50 or LC50 ≤ 1 mg/L) • Organic chemicals with low biodegradability (BOD28 < 20 %) • Organic chemicals or mixtures which meet 2 of the 3 following criteria: Biodegradability < 60 %, bioaccumulation potential (Log P OW ≥ 5), or toxicity of EC50 or LC50 ≤ 10 mg/L

• Priority list (Stortingsmelding Nr. 25) • OSPAR List of Chemicals for Priority Action • Both low biodegradability and high bioaccumulation (BOD28 < 20 %, and Log P OW ≥ 5) • Low biodegradability and toxic (BOD28 < 20 %, and EC50 or LC50 ≤ 10 mg/L) • Compounds expected to be carcinogenic/mutagenic or harmful to reproduction

• Inorganic chemicals with high toxicity (EC50 or LC50 ≤ 1 mg/L) • Organic chemicals with low biodegradability (BOD28 < 20 %) • Organic chemicals or mixtures which meet 2 of the 3 following criteria: Biodegradability < 60 %, bioaccumulation potential (Log P OW ≥ 5), or toxicity of EC50 or LC50 ≤ 10 mg/L
